chapter_context.summary: Declares that the Lord's hand is not shortened to save, but sins have separated the people, detailing their deep social wickedness and the Redeemer's covenant armor.

outline.text: Sins Separating People; Prevalence of Lies and Violence
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Sins Separating People; Prevalence of Lies and Violence (59:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Confession of Deep Spiritual Blindness and Lacking Justice
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Confession of Deep Spiritual Blindness and Lacking Justice (59:9-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Lord Arming Himself for Vengeance and Covenant Armor
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Lord Arming Himself for Vengeance and Covenant Armor (59:16-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Glory and Future Hope
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of glory and future hope, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 59, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
